evals.name: missing review scope
evals.prompt: A user says: review code. No diff, PR, branch, or files are specified. Describe the first response and workflow.
evals.expected_output: The response asks for review scope, offers uncommitted changes, branch vs main, or specific files, then plans diff/file reading and checks.
assertions: The output asks a clarifying question about review scope instead of choosing silently.
The output includes scope options such as uncommitted changes, branch comparison, or specific files.
The output says to inspect the relevant diff or files before making findings.
The output says findings must cite concrete files and lines or tool output.
The output includes running relevant checks when project tooling exists or saying what was skipped.

evals.name: security review large diff
evals.prompt: A user asks for a security-focused code review of a large mixed Go and TypeScript diff. Describe the review workflow and output.
evals.expected_output: The response scopes the diff, detects languages, optionally uses specialized reviewers, checks security risks with evidence, avoids web/private-code leakage, ranks severity, and reports concise file:line findings with fixes.
assertions: The output detects languages or areas in the changed files before choosing checks/reviewers.
The output mentions using specialized or parallel reviewers/subagents for a large diff.
The output focuses on security findings and ranks severity such as critical/important/suggestion.
Every finding format requires file and line evidence or explicit tool output.
The output warns not to paste private code into web tools and uses web only for external facts when needed.
The output includes concrete fixes for findings rather than vague observations.
